sail.FY2023-24: AMBIGUOUS: SAIL FY2022-23 PM is reported in concentration units only, not comparable to mass-based values

sections.about: Particulate Matter measures dust and fine particulate emissions from material handling, sintering, pelletising, coke making, BF/BOF/EAF operations, captive power and fugitive sources. This is disclosed under BRSR Principle 6, Essential Indicator for air emissions other than GHG emissions. A lower value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: Jindal Stainless led every year on PM, with 2.0841 kt in FY2022-23, 1.3133 kt in FY2023-24 and 1.6015 kt in FY2024-25. Tata improved consistently from 11 kt consolidated in FY2022-23 to 9 kt in FY2023-24 and 8 kt in FY2024-25, becoming the second-best performer in the latest year. JSW was relatively stable: 8.77 kt in FY2022-23, improving to 8.46 kt in FY2023-24 before a slight increase to 8.76 kt in FY2024-25. SAIL reported 11.16 kt in FY2023-24, improving to 10.74 kt in FY2024-25. Tata’s improvement aligns with the company’s stated modernisation, upgraded pollution-control equipment and significant reduction in stack dust emissions in India; Jindal also discloses APCDs, fugitive-emission management and continuous air-quality monitoring as mitigation measures.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata ranked 2nd on PM, behind Jindal Stainless. Tata’s 8 kt was 6.3985 kt higher than Jindal’s 1.6015 kt, or approximately 400% above the best performer. Tata’s ranking improved from 3rd in FY2022-23 and FY2023-24 to 2nd in FY2024-25. This is Tata’s strongest air-emissions signal in the set: despite integrated BF-BOF scale, dust performance improved year-on-year, likely reflecting the impact of stack-dust control, dedusting, maintenance discipline and modernised pollution-control assets.
sections.targets: Tata discloses dust-reduction investments and stack-dust mitigation.
sections.comparabilityNotes: Tata Steel FY2022-23 PM (11 kt) is a consolidated value. SAIL FY2022-23 reports PM in concentration units (µg/m³), which cannot be converted to mass without stack flow rate data and is excluded from the FY2023 peer comparison. JSW values across all three years are derived from intensity: FY2022-23: 0.42 kg/tcs × 20.87 Mt = 8.77 kt; FY2023-24: 0.38 kg/tcs × 22.26 Mt = 8.46 kt; FY2024-25: 0.39 kg/tcs × 22.46 Mt = 8.76 kt. SAIL FY2023-24 is derived from 0.58 kg/tcs × 19.24 Mt = 11.16 kt; FY2024-25 from 0.56 kg/tcs × 19.17 Mt = 10.74 kt. Jindal Stainless values are converted from metric tonnes to kilotonnes.

flags.flagNote: SAIL FY2022-23 PM is reported in concentration units only and cannot be converted to mass, so the FY2022-23 to FY2023-24 trend for SAIL is not assessable.
